name: Fillet Mosaic with Red Pepper Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast, peel, and puree red bell peppers.
2. Cut sole into 24 (1/2-inch-wide) strips, about 5 inches long.
3. Cut salmon fillets into 12 (1/2-inch-wide) strips, 5 inches long.
4. Trim stems from spinach leaves.
5. Blanch spinach in hot water for about 3 seconds to soften.
6. Drain and roll the spinach lengthwise as thinly as possible.
7. To prepare each serving, place 6 sole strips lengthwise side by side.
8. Tightly weave a spinach strip among the sole strips at the top.
9. Below, weave a salmon strip.
10. Now weave in another spinach strip.
11. Continue to alternately weave salmon and spinach strips.
12. You will need 3 salmon strips and 3 spinach strips for each sole mosaic.
13. Continue weaving the remaining portions.
14. Set aside.
15. Place stock and shallot in a skillet large enough to hold fish squares.
16. Bring to boil, then reduce heat.
17. Using a wide slotted spatula, lower fish squares into hot broth.
18. Cover and simmer over very low heat for 5 minutes.
19. Carefully remove the fish squares and keep warm.
20. If your skillet is not large enough to hold all of the fish, cook them in batches.
21. Skim any white residue from the remaining broth in the skillet.
22. Add wine to the broth and simmer over high heat until it reduces by half.
23. Stir in red pepper puree, apple jelly, and lemon juice and season to taste with salt and pepper.
24. Strain the sauce.
25. Place some of the sauce on the bottom of serving platter or individual plates.
26. Cover with sole squares.
